description: Add rich text editing for event descriptions, replacing the plain textarea with a WYSIWYG editor that supports formatting (bold, italic, lists, links, headings) and renders styled HTML on the public event page.

userStories.title: Integrate a lightweight rich text editor component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable rich text editor component so that it can be used in the event creation form and any future forms requiring formatted text input.
acceptanceCriteria: A new RichTextEditor component exists at src/components/RichTextEditor.tsx
Uses Tiptap (headless, built on ProseMirror) as the editor library — install @tiptap/react, @tiptap/starter-kit, and @tiptap/extension-link
Supports bold, italic, bullet list, ordered list, headings (H2, H3), and links
Includes a minimal formatting toolbar with icon buttons for each action
Component accepts value (HTML string) and onChange props for controlled usage
Component is marked 'use client' since it requires browser APIs
Toolbar buttons show active state when cursor is inside formatted text
TypeScript strict mode passes with no errors (npm run build)

userStories.title: Use rich text editor in event creation form
userStories.description: As an event host, I want to write a formatted description when creating an event so that I can highlight important details with bold text, lists, and links.
acceptanceCriteria: The event creation form at src/app/create/ replaces the plain textarea for description with the RichTextEditor component
The description field stores HTML string in the Supabase events table description column (existing text column, no migration needed)
Form validation still requires a non-empty description before submission
The API route at src/app/api/events/ accepts and stores the HTML description
HTML is sanitized server-side before storage using DOMPurify (install isomorphic-dompurify) to strip script tags and dangerous attributes
Existing events with plain text descriptions continue to display correctly
TypeScript strict mode passes with no errors (npm run build)
E2E test in e2e/event-creation.spec.ts verifies the rich text editor renders on the create page

userStories.title: Render rich text description on public event page
userStories.description: As an event guest, I want to see the formatted event description on the public event page so that I can easily read structured information about the event.
acceptanceCriteria: The public event page at src/app/e/[slug]/ renders the description as styled HTML instead of plain text
HTML output is sanitized before rendering using DOMPurify to prevent XSS
Rendered HTML uses Tailwind prose classes (via @tailwindcss/typography plugin) for consistent typography styling
Install and configure @tailwindcss/typography in tailwind.config.ts if not already present
Bold, italic, lists, headings, and links render with appropriate visual styling
Links in the description open in a new tab with rel='noopener noreferrer'
Plain text descriptions (legacy events without HTML) still render correctly as paragraphs
TypeScript strict mode passes with no errors (npm run build)
E2E test in e2e/public-event.spec.ts verifies formatted description renders with correct HTML elements (e.g., bold text appears in a <strong> tag)

userStories.title: Render rich text description on host dashboard
userStories.description: As an event host, I want to see the formatted description on my dashboard event detail view so that I can verify how my description looks.
acceptanceCriteria: The dashboard event detail view at src/app/dashboard/ renders the description as styled HTML using the same prose styling as the public page
HTML output is sanitized before rendering using DOMPurify
Reuses the same rendering approach (Tailwind prose classes) as the public event page for consistency
TypeScript strict mode passes with no errors (npm run build)
E2E tests continue to pass (npm test)
